metadata.name: pysaal

methods.name: pysaal.lib._dlls.DLLs.open_log_file
documentation:
Opens a log file for writing.

```
.. notes::
    * The log file will be created if it does not exist.
    * If the log file exists, it will be overwritten.
    * close_log_file() should be called when finished with the log.
    * Due to memory usage, the log should only be used for debugging purposes.
```

```
param filename

Path to the log file.
```

classes.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetEquinoctial

methods.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetEquinoctial.from_keplerian
documentation:
Converts from Keplerian elements to Equinoctial elements

```
param kep

The Keplerian elements to convert

example

```

```python
from pysaal.elements import ConvertElements, KeplerianElements

kep = KeplerianElements(42164, 0.0001, 0.42, 42, 200, 300)
eqnx = ConvertElements.equinoctial.from_keplerian(kep)
```

methods.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetEquinoctial.from_classical
documentation:
Converts from Classical elements to Equinoctial elements

```
param cl

The Classical elements to convert

example

```

```python
from pysaal.elements import ConvertElements, ClassicalElements

cl = ClassicalElements(
    1.0027444112324284,
    0.0001,
    0.42,
    42,
    200,
    300,
)
eqnx = ConvertElements.equinoctial.from_classical(cl)
```

methods.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetEquinoctial.from_cartesian
documentation:
Converts from Cartesian elements to Equinoctial elements

```
param cart

The TEME cartesian elements in  and 

param mu

The gravitational parameter of the central body in 

example

```

```python
from pysaal.elements import ConvertElements, CartesianElements
from pysaal.bodies import Earth

cart = CartesianElements(
    -42134.866555365596,
    -1477.3611216130757,
    -95.4631435846219,
    0.10749110300482166,
    -3.072941987275631,
    0.021437245884576635,
)
eqnx = ConvertElements.equinoctial.from_cartesian(cart, Earth.get_mu())
```

Class used to calculate the equinoctial elements of a satellite orbit

:::{note}
This class is not intended to be used directly. Use the {class}`ConvertElements` class instead.
:::

classes.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetKeplerian

methods.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetKeplerian.from_equinoctial
documentation:
Converts from Equinoctial elements to Keplerian elements

```
param eqnx

The Equinoctial elements to convert

example

```

```python
from pysaal.elements import ConvertElements, EquinoctialElements

eqnx = EquinoctialElements(
    -7.660444431189783e-05,
    6.42787609686539e-05, -0.001253574911285049,
    -0.0034441687623505044,
    542.0000000000001,
    1.0027444112324284
)
kep = ConvertElements.keplerian.from_equinoctial(eqnx)
```

methods.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetKeplerian.from_cartesian
documentation:
Converts from Cartesian elements to Keplerian elements

```
param cart

The cartesian elements in  and 

param mu

The gravitational parameter of the central body in 

example

```

```python
from pysaal.elements import ConvertElements, CartesianElements
from pysaal.bodies import Earth

cart = CartesianElements(
    -42134.866555365596,
    -1477.3611216130757,
    -95.4631435846219,
    0.10749110300482166,
    -3.072941987275631,
    0.021437245884576635,
)
kep = ConvertElements.keplerian.from_cartesian(cart, Earth.get_mu())
```

Class used to calculate the keplerian elements of a satellite orbit

:::{note}
This class is not intended to be used directly. Use the {class}`ConvertElements` class instead.
:::

classes.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetTLE

methods.name: pysaal.elements._convert_elements._GetTLE.from_sp_vector
documentation:
Converts from a SPVector to a TLE

```
param sp

The SPVector to convert

param tle_type

The type of TLE to generate

return

The TLE of the satellite

example

```

```python
from pysaal.elements import ConvertElements, SPVector, CartesianElements
from pysaal.time import Epoch
from pysaal.enums import TLEType

epoch = Epoch.now()
teme_state = CartesianElements(
    -42134.866555365596,
    -1477.3611216130757,
    -95.4631435846219,
    0.10749110300482166,
    -3.072941987275631,
    0.021437245884576635,
)
sat_id = 1
sp = SPVector(epoch, teme_state, sat_id)
tle_type = TLEType.SGP4
tle = ConvertElements.tle.from_sp_vector(sp, tle_type)
```

Class used to calculate the TLE of a satellite orbit

:::{note}
This class is not intended to be used directly. Use the {class}`ConvertElements` class instead.
:::

classes.name: pysaal.elements._tle.TLE

methods.name: pysaal.elements._tle.TLE.from_lines
documentation:
Create a TLE object from two TLE lines.

```
param line_1

The first line of the TLE.

param line_2

The second line of the TLE.
```

:::{note}
This is the preferred method for creating a TLE object.
:::

```
example

```

```python
from pysaal.elements import TLE

line_1 = "1 25544U 98067A   24340.99323416 +.00018216  00000 0  32316-3 0 0999"
line_2 = "2 25544  51.6388 184.2057 0007028 306.7642 201.1123 15.5026597648519"

tle = TLE.from_lines(line_1, line_2)

print(tle.satellite_id)
```

methods.name: pysaal.bodies._sun.Sun.get_analytic_position
documentation:
Get the position of the Sun using an analytic model. $(km)$

```
param epoch

The epoch at which to calculate the position.
```

methods.name: pysaal.bodies._sun.Sun.get_jpl_position
documentation:
Get the position of the Sun using the JPL ephemeris. $(km)$

```
param epoch

The epoch at which to calculate the position.
```


methods.name: pysaal.bodies._earth.Earth._get_constant
documentation:
Get an environment constant by index.

:::{note}
This method is not intended to be used directly. Instead, most constants have been exposed as methods.
:::
